page.title: Labour Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: A May Day Adventure at Sea

data.meta_description: Celebrate Labour Day with a yacht charter in Marbella. Escape the crowds on a private boat from Puerto Banús from €749/2h. Book your May Day adventure now.
data.summary: Labour Day in Marbella is the perfect time to escape the crowds with a private yacht charter. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús with licensed skippers, drinks, and snacks included, starting at €749 for two hours.
key_facts: Labour Day yacht charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Private boat hire avoids crowded beaches and restaurants on May Day.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) include a free jet ski, from €4,719 for 4 hours.
Suitable for groups up to 12 pax on most yachts; smaller speedboats for 5-6 pax.
Popular destinations: Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Cala del Faro.
Book early – Labour Day is a busy holiday in Spain.

faq.q: What is included in a Labour Day yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: How much does a Labour Day yacht charter cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) start at €4,719 for 4 hours and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request.

faq.q: Which boats are available for Labour Day in Marbella?
faq.a: Our fleet includes 27 boats from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. Popular choices for Labour Day are the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on a Labour Day charter?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our packages already include a selection of beverages and light snacks, but you can supplement as you wish.

faq.q: What are the best destinations for a Labour Day yacht charter from Marbella?
faq.a: Popular routes include cruising to Cabopino, anchoring at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, visiting Estepona, or heading to Sotogrande. Your skipper will recommend the best spots based on weather and sea conditions.

faq.q: Is Labour Day a busy time for yacht charters in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, Labour Day (1 May) is a public holiday in Spain, so demand is high. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance to secure your preferred boat and time slot.

faq.q: How many people can join a Labour Day yacht charter?
faq.a: Capacity varies by boat. Most yachts accommodate up to 12 passengers (e.g., Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94). Smaller boats like the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are ideal for intimate groups.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for Labour Day charters?
faq.a: We offer flexible cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. For Labour Day bookings, we recommend checking the specific terms at the time of booking via WhatsApp.

data.body_html:
Labour Day in Marbella is a public holiday, and everyone heads to the coast. Beaches fill up, restaurants book out, and the Golden Mile gets gridlocked. The smart alternative? A Labour Day yacht charter in Marbella. Escape the crowds, anchor in a quiet cove, and enjoy the Costa del Sol from the water. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús with licensed skippers, drinks, and snacks included. Prices start at €749 for two hours on a Tier A boat. Here is everything you need to plan your May Day adventure.

Why choose a Labour Day yacht charter in Marbella?

May Day is a national holiday in Spain. That means locals and tourists alike flood the coast. By booking a private boat, you skip the queues, the packed beaches, and the overpriced sunbeds. You get total privacy, a dedicated crew, and the freedom to set your own itinerary. Whether you want to sunbathe, swim, or explore hidden coves, a yacht charter in Marbella delivers a stress-free day out.

Our fleet for Labour Day

We operate 27 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from nimble speedboats to superyachts. Here are the most popular choices for Labour Day:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h or €2,299/8h
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – same pricing as Astondoa
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – from €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – pricing on request
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – for a luxury Labour Day experience
  • Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) – budget-friendly option

All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA. No surprises.

What is included in your Labour Day charter?

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. Fuel is covered. You get a selection of water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava, plus light snacks. Insurance and safety equipment are included. Spanish VAT (21%) is in the price. There are no add-ons or hidden fees. Just turn up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

Best destinations for Labour Day from Marbella

Your skipper will plan the route based on conditions, but popular options include:

  • Cala del Faro – a quiet cove near Cabopino, ideal for swimming
  • Cala Cortés – another secluded spot with crystal-clear water
  • Estepona – a charming marina with tapas bars
  • Sotogrande – a luxury port with upscale dining
  • Puerto Banús itself – anchor near the port and watch the yachts go by

Most Labour Day trips last 4-6 hours, giving you time to visit two or three spots.

Labour Day weather in Marbella

Early May in Marbella is warm and sunny. Average highs reach 24°C, sea temperatures hover around 18°C, and there are roughly 10 hours of daylight. Light breeze is common, making for comfortable cruising. Rain is rare. It is an ideal window for a Labour Day yacht charter in Marbella.

How to book your Labour Day charter

Labour Day is one of the busiest days of the year. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance. To secure your preferred boat, contact us via WhatsApp with your group size, preferred time, and budget. We will confirm availability and send a booking link. Payment is secure, and cancellation is free up to 48 hours before departure.

Tips for a perfect Labour Day at sea

  • Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat – the sun is strong even in May.
  • Pack a swimsuit and towel – you will want to jump in.
  • Arrive 15 minutes early at Puerto Banús for boarding.
  • If you want a jet ski, book a Tier B boat (Mangusta 80) – it is included free.
  • Consider a full-day charter (8 hours) to explore further afield.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a Labour Day yacht charter in Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

How much does a Labour Day yacht charter cost in Marbella?

Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) start at €4,719 for 4 hours and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request.

Which boats are available for Labour Day in Marbella?

Our fleet includes 27 boats from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. Popular choices for Labour Day are the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a Labour Day charter?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our packages already include a selection of beverages and light snacks, but you can supplement as you wish.

What are the best destinations for a Labour Day yacht charter from Marbella?

Popular routes include cruising to Cabopino, anchoring at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, visiting Estepona, or heading to Sotogrande. Your skipper will recommend the best spots based on weather and sea conditions.

Is Labour Day a busy time for yacht charters in Marbella?

Yes, Labour Day (1 May) is a public holiday in Spain, so demand is high. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance to secure your preferred boat and time slot.

How many people can join a Labour Day yacht charter?

Capacity varies by boat. Most yachts accommodate up to 12 passengers (e.g., Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94). Smaller boats like the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are ideal for intimate groups.

What is the cancellation policy for Labour Day charters?

We offer flexible cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. For Labour Day bookings, we recommend checking the specific terms at the time of booking via WhatsApp.
